_provenance.stage: Stage 1 — derived from live cards for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Bumiputera Discount on Property Costs Non-Bumiputera Buyers RM4.8B
context: The mandatory 5-15% Bumiputera property discount, required by all state governments, cost non-Bumiputera buyers an estimated RM4.8 billion in higher prices in 2024 as developers cross-subsidised.

cards.text: Bumiputera property discounts range from 5% to 15% across states.
cards.sub: Developers recover the discount by raising prices for non-Bumiputera buyers. The subsidy is cross-funded, not government-funded.

cards.h: Average Bumiputera lot take-up rate is 54%.
cards.s: 46% of discounted units are not purchased and must be released, after 6-18 months of market delay.
cards.d: Average Bumiputera lot take-up rate is 54%. 46% of discounted units are not purchased and must be released, after 6-18 months of market delay.

cards.h: No means-testing applies to the Bumiputera discount.
cards.s: A T20 Bumiputera household receives the same discount as a B40 household.
cards.d: No means-testing applies to the Bumiputera discount. A T20 Bumiputera household receives the same discount as a B40 household.

cards.h: Singapore's ethnic housing quotas apply to buyers AND sellers equally.
cards.s: Malaysia's system benefits one group at another's direct expense, with no reciprocal obligation.
cards.d: Singapore's ethnic housing quotas apply to buyers AND sellers equally. Malaysia's system benefits one group at another's direct expense, with no reciprocal obligation.

cards.text: A discount funded by non-Bumiputera buyers is not a government subsidy. It is a private tax on one group for another.
Like a coupon that one group gets free, except it is funded by charging the other group extra at checkout
cards.sub: The discount is not a government expenditure; it is a cross-subsidy where non-Bumiputera buyers absorb the cost through higher prices

cards.text: If Bumiputera housing is a national priority, fund it from national revenue, not cross-subsidy. The current mechanism creates resentment because its costs are visible and unequal.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
